text: The default mode network is not a network of distraction — it is a network of self. Neuroscientist Mary Helen Immordino-Yang has argued that its core function is autobiographical simulation: rehearsing who we are, who we might become, and how our actions connect to other people. When you stare out a window and your mind drifts to a past conversation or an imagined reunion, the DMN is doing deep social and moral processing. The problem is not the network itself but its poor timing — firing during moments that demand directed attention, pulling focus toward the interior at the expense of the external task.
question: According to Mary Helen Immordino-Yang, what is the core function of the default mode network?

options.text: Consolidating short-term memories into long-term storage
